author: @XyroAMVs123
text:
Hey brother 😭🙏 I gotta ask, why didn’t you use Tiki Phonk this time? 🗿 Honestly, Tiki Phonk would’ve matched the whole vibe perfectly and made the edit way more energetic ⚡🔥 The bass, the cowbells, and the overall atmosphere would’ve gone absolutely crazy 🎧💥 I was genuinely expecting that style because it always makes the clips feel smoother, harder, and way more satisfying to watch 👌💯
Don’t get me wrong though, the edit was still clean 🍷 but Tiki Phonk would’ve pushed it to another level entirely 🚀 The way those distorted beats sync with transitions is unmatched 😭 Every flash, zoom, and movement would’ve hit ten times harder 💣 The music basically gives edits an aura 👁️🗿 Like the second the beat drops, everything suddenly feels cinematic and dangerous 😈⚔️Brother, your editing style already fits Tiki Phonk perfectly too 🤝 The pacing, the transitions, the effects — all of it screams for that aggressive energy 🔥 Imagine the bass dropping exactly when the clips switch 💥 or the cowbells echoing during the slow motion parts 🎶 That would’ve been legendary 😭🙏 People definitely would’ve replayed the edit over and over because the vibe would’ve been addicting 📈🌀
That’s the thing with Tiki Phonk 🎧 It doesn’t just sound cool — it transforms the whole experience 🌌 Even simple clips start feeling powerful and memorable 🗿 The atmosphere becomes darker, cleaner, and way more hype ⚡ Without it, the edit still looks good, but with it? Brother… it becomes generational 🍷🔥
Next time, trust the vision and let the Tiki Phonk carry the edit 😭🙏 Let the bass shake the screen 💣 let the transitions breathe with the beat 🎶 and let the aura consume the entire video 👁️🗿 Trust me brother, everything will sound cleaner, hit harder, and feel way more unforgettable 💯
Capiche boy? 🍷🗿

author: @codyrafferty9659
text: Not only do the Knicks play the game like a true team where nobody is bigger or has such a huge ego that its all about them but they also are all humble and not braggadocious. They are cool and calm and chill. They stay cool under pressure which seemed like it was different than how the spurs and especially Wemby played like. This is most likely a sweep with the atmosphere in the Garden. That place will have the energy that hasn't been seen in a playoff game in a long long time if ever. As a lifelong Knicks fan of 45 years this is like a dream. This is for the greats of the Knicks that never won a finals but in my opinion deserved it. Ewing, Starks, Houston, Oakley, Carmelo among others. What time to be a fan of theirs and to be alive! 2 more games baby LFG!!!!!

For those who don’t understand
The batter was struck out so that was the second of the three outs, but the catcher thought it was the third out. author: @angryginger791
text: So, my son (5) is a big DudePerfect fan. He loves all the crazy challenges and trick shots. I have to thank you guys because he has learned some valuable traits from your channel. He is not afraid of seemingly impossible challenges. Instead of thinking, "I can't do that." he thinks, "This might take A LOT of tries." He's starting to understand the importance of practice and training. He doesn't get upset if he makes a mistake or fails, he just wants to try again. It's really awesome to see, and I hope he applies it to everything in his life. So, keep up the great work, and big thanks for being a positive influence.

author: @alkers372
text: Russell Crowe stated that many years ago he was watching young men play rugby in England between takes of the movie "Proof of Life".  He said one player really stood out to Russell because of his fluidity and athleticism, and they talked together afterwards.  He said he was surprised that the only thing the young man wanted to talk about was acting.  Years later that young man came up to Crowe and asked if he remembered meeting him.  That man was Henry Cavill, who he co-stared with him in "Man of Steel."

author: @sudsysutherland359
text: Good ole Michigan Super Speedway. I live in Windsor,Ontario,Canada which is right across the river from the “Motor City” in Detroit & Michigan is my home track & i’ve been there in 97-01-03 when Nascar was peaking & 135,000 fans back then & they raced there in June & August 🏁 seeing my Dodge Boys back in Nascar in 2001 for their inaugural season was epic & my boy #40 Sterling Marlin in the Coors Lite Dodge Intrepid was so awesome! I was wearing my Mopar Performance hat & was waving it around beside my buddy who was a Jeff Gordon fan & my other buddy a Dale Earnhardt Jr. fan & i called it on our drive to Michigan that it was going to be a Mopar day! & they looked at me like i had three heads & saying it’s there first season calm down & i’m like Dodge came off the hauler locked & loaded & as most people know who are old enough to remember that Sterling Marlin finished 3rd in Dodge’s inaugural season in 2001 & in 2002 he was on a mission for that 2002 championship & leading the points for most of the season until he fractured his neck vertebrae forcing him to miss the remainder of the season & a young Jamie McMurray in only his second cup race ever won the “Coca-Cola 600” at Charlotte & i remember Chip Ganassi coming over to Jamie McMurray with the cell phone in victory lane & congratulating Jamie on his big win in which must have been hard for Sterling because he knew that championship was there’s in 2002 & he was on one of those Jeff Gordon domination seasons 🏁

author: @AlexanderGuizzotti-r4w
text: You see these guys names and hear them so often you think “they will be in the NFL no problem”. Reality is, in my sport of hockey, only 4 guys I know, ever even got drafted in to the NHL and that’s 20,000+ guys I’ve played against in my career since age 3 starting. Only 1 of those 4, ever spent more than 1 full season with an NHL team and one of those other guys, captained a very popular college hockey team and the only 2-3 games in the NHL he ever played in, were because a bruins star was injured temporarily. He scored and got an assist and that’s the last time he ever got called up to play in the NHL in his entire career.   It’s not just being good enough, it’s being the right fit for the play style, for coaching styles, for managements view of the future. Even small things you guys don’t consider because it’s not your life, losing girlfriends is really painful. I opted out of professional hockey because my girlfriend didn’t want to live 2.5 hours from me and have me travel 8 months out of the year with the team with no contract guarantee. Once I lost my girlfriend, I lost my focus, my passion for the future games. I opted out, went down a different path. Unless you earn that money consistently, most women can’t follow you or afford to leave their lives for you. Most guys on practice squad, never get in the game, never even have secure futures on practice squad period.    Pro-level is an entirely different life, different dog in you that has to survive.
